meta.description: Plain-English definitions for words used throughout HumanityOS
meta.usage: Loaded by glossary.js for in-app tooltip definitions

words.avatar: A digital representation of a person in a virtual world
words.bandwidth: The amount of data that can be transferred in a given time
words.binary: A system using only two values: 0 and 1. Also refers to compiled executable files
words.cache: Temporary storage that speeds up repeated access to data
words.compile: Convert human-readable code into machine-executable instructions
words.database: An organized collection of data stored and accessed electronically
words.deploy: Release software to a server or platform where users can access it
words.encrypt: Convert data into a coded format that only authorized parties can read
words.endpoint: A specific URL where an API can be accessed
words.federation: A system where independent servers cooperate while maintaining autonomy
words.hash: A fixed-size value computed from input data. Same input always produces same hash
words.instance: A single running copy of software or a single occurrence of an object
words.latency: The delay between sending a request and receiving a response
words.metadata: Data that describes other data (like file size, creation date, author)
words.node: A single point in a network that can send, receive, or relay data
words.payload: The actual data being transmitted, excluding headers and protocol overhead
words.protocol: A set of rules governing how data is transmitted between systems
words.query: A request for specific data from a database or API
words.relay: A server that forwards messages between users without storing them permanently
words.render: Convert data into a visual display (draw graphics on screen)
words.repository: A central place where code and its history are stored (like GitHub)
words.runtime: The period during which a program is executing
words.schema: The structure or blueprint defining how data is organized
words.serialize: Convert a data structure into a format that can be stored or transmitted
words.server: A computer that provides services to other computers over a network
words.session: A period of interaction between a user and a system
words.socket: An endpoint for sending and receiving data across a network
words.sync: Make two or more copies of data identical by transferring changes
words.thread: A sequence of instructions that can run independently within a program
words.token: A piece of data that represents something else (access permission, digital asset)
words.viewport: The visible area of a web page or game window
